title: Isotrema salvadorense
intro: Isotrema salvadorense, also known as the "Mazatec snuff plant," is a species of flowering plant in the bignoniacae family native to the Sierra Mazateca region of Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Isotrema salvadorense include alkaloids, such as isosalvadorine and isosalvadorinine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make a snuff, as well as teas and infusions, which are said to have a range of medicinal effects, including inducing lucid dreaming and promoting spiritual awareness.
